name: Stuffed Onions
recipeIngredient: 6 Large Vidalia Onions or 8 medium yellow onions
2 tablespoons fresh marjoram, minced (used 1 teaspoon dried)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Cut off about 1/4 of the root end from both ends of the onions, leaving only the starchy layers. Place onions in a baking dish, root end down, and bake for 30 minutes or until golden brown. Let cool, then hollow out each onion, leaving a 1/2-inch shell (outermost 2-4 layers).
3. In a medium hot pan, chop 1 cup of reserved onion and sauté with mushrooms for 3 minutes. Add garlic and apple, and cook for an additional 3 minutes. Stir in parsley, marjoram, lemon juice, paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix well with cooked rice.
4. Fill each onion shell with the rice mixture. Pour enough stock to cover the bottom of the onions (about 1/2 inch). Bake for 20 minutes, basting occasionally.
Makes 6-8 servings depending on the size of the onions used.
